Purpose: Defines the visual theme and layout foundation for the game.
Snippet: css html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; background: #faf8ef; color: #776e65; font-family: "Clear Sans", "Helvetica Neue", Arial, sans-serif; }
Explanation: Establishes the core visual identity with a warm beige background (#faf8ef) and brownish text (#776e65). The Clear Sans font provides a modern, clean aesthetic appropriate for the minimalist game design.

Purpose: Animates the floating score addition indicators.
Snippet: css @keyframes move-up { 0% { top: 25px; opacity: 1; } 100% { top: -50px; opacity: 0; } }
Explanation: Creates a 600ms animation that floats score additions upward from their starting position while fading out. Used exclusively for the "+X" score indicators that appear after merging tiles.

Purpose: Provides smooth appearance for game-over/win messages.
Snippet: css @keyframes fade-in { 0% { opacity: 0; } 100% { opacity: 1; } }
Explanation: A simple opacity transition applied to the game-message overlay. Delays 1200ms to allow users to see the final tile merge before the message appears.

Purpose: Animates new tile spawning with scale effect.
Snippet: css @keyframes appear { 0% { opacity: 0; transform: scale(0); } 100% { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); } }
Explanation: Creates a 200ms pop-in effect for newly generated tiles (2 or 4). The scale transformation grows from 0% to 100% size, making tiles feel like they're materializing onto the grid.

Purpose: Animates tile merging with bounce effect.
Snippet: css @keyframes pop { 0% { transform: scale(0); } 50% { transform: scale(1.2); } 100% { transform: scale(1); } }
Explanation: Implements a 200ms bounce animation when two tiles merge. The overshoot to 120% scale creates a satisfying "pop" that emphasizes the merge action and point gain.

Purpose: Shows the current game score with label.
Snippet: css .score-container:after { content: "Score"; }
Explanation: Uses a CSS pseudo-element to inject the "Score" label above the numeric value. The :after technique keeps the HTML clean while maintaining semantic separation of label and value.

Purpose: Displays the persistent high score across sessions.
Snippet: css .best-container:after { content: "Best"; }
Explanation: Similar to score-container but tracks the dimension-specific best score via localStorage. The label is injected via CSS, and the value persists with key format bestScore_WxH.

Purpose: Floating point indicators for score increases.
Snippet: css .score-addition { position: absolute; right: 30px; color: rgba(119, 110, 101, 0.9); animation: move-up 600ms ease-in; }
Explanation: Positioned absolutely within the score container to float upward. The semi-transparent brown color matches the theme while being distinct enough to read during the animation.

Purpose: Creates the dynamically-sized main game board.
Snippet: css .game-container { margin: 40px auto 0; padding: 15px; background: #bbada0; border-radius: 6px; box-sizing: border-box; }
Explanation: Establishes the beige-brown game board with rounded corners and centered horizontal alignment. Dimensions are set dynamically via JavaScript based on selected grid size (updateGridSize method).

Purpose: Modal overlay for win/loss conditions.
Snippet: css .game-message { display: none; position: absolute; top: 0; right: 0; bottom: 0; left: 0; background: rgba(238, 228, 218, 0.5); z-index: 100; }
Explanation: Initially hidden (display: none), becomes visible when .game-won or .game-over classes are added. Semi-transparent overlay prevents interaction with the grid while showing the message.

Purpose: Background cells that show the empty grid structure.
Snippet: css .grid-cell { margin-right: 15px; float: left; border-radius: 3px; background: rgba(238, 228, 218, 0.35); }
Explanation: Creates the subtle beige cells visible behind the tiles. Dimensions are set dynamically by JavaScript (updateGridSize) to match the selected grid configuration, maintaining 15px gutters.

Purpose: Common styles for all tile objects.
Snippet: css .tile { position: absolute; transition: 100ms ease-in-out; transition-property: transform; } .tile .tile-inner { border-radius: 3px; text-align: center; font-weight: bold; }
Explanation: Tiles are absolutely positioned within the tile-container for precise control. The 100ms transition creates smooth sliding animations. Dimensions and font sizes are set dynamically via injected CSS styles.

Purpose: Light beige tiles for starting values.
Snippet: css .tile.tile-2 .tile-inner { background: #eee4da; } .tile.tile-4 .tile-inner { background: #ede0c8; }
Explanation: The two lowest values use light, neutral backgrounds with dark text. These are the most common tiles, so they use subtle colors that don't dominate the visual hierarchy.

Purpose: Orange gradient for mid-range values.
Snippet: css .tile.tile-8 .tile-inner { color: #f9f6f2; background: #f2b179; } .tile.tile-64 .tile-inner { background: #f65e3b; }
Explanation: As tile values increase, colors shift from light orange to deep red-orange. Text switches to white (#f9f6f2) for contrast against darker backgrounds.

Purpose: Golden tiles with glow effects for high values.
Snippet: css .tile.tile-128 .tile-inner { background: #edcf72; box-shadow: 0 0 30px 10px rgba(243, 215, 116, 0.2381); }
Explanation: High-value tiles get golden backgrounds with progressive glow effects (increasing alpha on box-shadow). These tiles use dynamically calculated smaller font sizes to fit larger numbers.

Purpose: Dark tiles for values exceeding 2048.
Snippet: css .tile.tile-super .tile-inner { color: #f9f6f2; background: #3c3a32; }
Explanation: Once players exceed 2048, tiles switch to a dark charcoal background. Font size is dynamically calculated at 30% of cell size to accommodate 4-5 digit numbers.

Purpose: Prominent button to reset the game.
Snippet: css .restart-button { display: inline-block; background: #8f7a66; border-radius: 3px; padding: 0 20px; color: #f9f6f2; height: 40px; line-height: 42px; float: right; }
Explanation: Brown button with white text, floated to the right for consistent placement. The 40px height matches the game-intro text line-height for visual alignment.

Purpose: Full-width button below game grid for undo functionality.
Snippet: css .undo-button { background: #8f7a66; color: #f9f6f2; height: 40px; margin: 20px auto 0; width: 100%; max-width: 500px; display: block; }
Explanation: Centered button using auto margins with maximum width constraint. Styled to match the restart button aesthetic while having distinct block-level positioning below the game area.

Purpose: Visual feedback when undo is unavailable.
Snippet: css .undo-button:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }
Explanation: Reduces opacity to 50% and changes cursor when no moves are available to undo. Provides clear visual feedback that the button is non-interactive.

Purpose: Interactive feedback on undo button hover.
Snippet: css .undo-button:not(:disabled):hover { background: #9f8a76; }
Explanation: Lightens the background color when hovering over an enabled undo button. The :not(:disabled) selector ensures hover only applies when the button is active.

Purpose: Control panel for selecting grid dimensions.
Snippet: css .grid-size-selector { margin: 20px auto 0; padding: 20px; background: #bbada0; border-radius: 6px; max-width: 760px; }
Explanation: Matches the game container aesthetic with same background color. Centered with auto margins and maximum width for visual consistency across viewport sizes.

Purpose: Title for the grid size controls.
Snippet: css .grid-size-selector h3 { margin: 0 0 15px 0; color: #f9f6f2; font-size: 16px; text-transform: uppercase; }
Explanation: Light text on dark background with uppercase transformation for visual hierarchy. 15px bottom margin provides spacing before the preset buttons.

Purpose: Flex layout for quick grid size selection.
Snippet: css .preset-buttons { display: flex; gap: 10px; flex-wrap: wrap; margin-bottom: 15px; }
Explanation: Uses flexbox with 10px gap between buttons and wrapping enabled for responsive behavior. The flex-wrap ensures buttons flow to new line on narrow viewports.

Purpose: Individual preset size buttons (3×3, 4×4, etc.).
Snippet: css .preset-btn { background: #8f7a66; color: #f9f6f2; border: none; border-radius: 3px; padding: 8px 16px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: bold; cursor: pointer; }
Explanation: Styled to match restart/undo button aesthetic. Bold font and adequate padding ensure good touch targets. Data attributes (data-width, data-height) store dimension values.

Purpose: Interactive feedback on button hover.
Snippet: css .preset-btn:hover { background: #9f8a76; }
Explanation: Lightens background color on hover to provide visual feedback. Consistent with other interactive elements in the interface.

Purpose: Visual indicator for currently selected grid size.
Snippet: css .preset-btn.active { background: #776e65; }
Explanation: Darker background color distinguishes the active grid configuration. Applied/removed via JavaScript when preset buttons are clicked or custom dimensions applied.

Purpose: Layout for width/height input controls.
Snippet: css .custom-grid-inputs { display: flex; gap: 15px; align-items: center; margin-top: 15px; padding-top: 15px; border-top: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.2); }
Explanation: Flexbox layout with 15px gap between input groups. Top border separates custom controls from preset buttons. Aligns items vertically to center for consistent baseline.

Purpose: Label and input field pairing for width/height.
Snippet: css .input-group { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 5px; }
Explanation: Vertical flex layout stacks label above input with 5px gap. Creates clear visual association between label and control.

Purpose: Labels for width and height inputs.
Snippet: css .input-group label { color: #f9f6f2; font-size: 12px; font-weight: bold; text-transform: uppercase; }
Explanation: Light text on dark background with uppercase transformation. Small font size (12px) distinguishes labels from primary UI text while maintaining readability.

Purpose: Number input controls for custom dimensions.
Snippet: css .input-group input { width: 60px; padding: 8px; border: none; border-radius: 3px; font-size: 16px; font-weight: bold; text-align: center; }
Explanation: Compact 60px width with centered text and bold font. Border-radius matches other UI elements. Type="number" with min="3" max="10" attributes enforce valid range.

Purpose: Action button to confirm custom grid dimensions.
Snippet: css .apply-custom-btn { background: #4ade80; color: #111827; border: none; border-radius: 3px; padding: 8px 20px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: bold; cursor: pointer; margin-top: 20px; }
Explanation: Distinctive green background (#4ade80) signals positive action. Dark text provides contrast. Positioned with top margin to separate from input fields.

Purpose: Interactive feedback on apply button hover.
Snippet: css .apply-custom-btn:hover { background: #22c55e; }
Explanation: Darker green on hover maintains visual consistency with other interactive elements. Provides clear affordance that the button is clickable.

Purpose: Shows current grid dimensions.
Snippet: css .grid-info { margin-top: 10px; color: #f9f6f2; font-size: 14px; }
Explanation: Light text displays current configuration (e.g., "Current grid: 4×4"). Updated dynamically via JavaScript (textContent) when grid size changes.

Purpose: Adapts layout for screens under 520px.
Snippet: css @media screen and (max-width: 520px) { .preset-btn { padding: 6px 12px; font-size: 12px; } .custom-grid-inputs { flex-direction: column; align-items: stretch; } }
Explanation: Reduces preset button size and changes custom inputs to vertical stacking on narrow viewports. Ensures usability on mobile devices while maintaining visual hierarchy.

Purpose: Adds ES5 bind() support for legacy browsers.
Snippet: js if (!Function.prototype.bind) { Function.prototype.bind = function(oThis) { var aArgs = Array.prototype.slice.call(arguments, 1); ... }; }
Explanation: Implements the .bind() method for browsers older than IE9. Allows preservation of 'this' context when passing methods as callbacks, essential for event handlers throughout the game.

Purpose: Cross-browser animation timing support.
Snippet: js for(var x = 0; x < vendors.length && !window.requestAnimationFrame; ++x) { window.requestAnimationFrame = window[vendors[x]+'RequestAnimationFrame']; }
Explanation: Checks for vendor-prefixed implementations (ms, moz, webkit, o) before falling back to setTimeout-based timing. Ensures 60 FPS animation on all browsers including older mobile Safari.

Purpose: In-memory fallback for localStorage unavailability.
Snippet: js window.fakeStorage = { _data: {}, setItem: function(id, val) { return this._data[id] = String(val); }, getItem: function(id) { return this._data.hasOwnProperty(id) ? this._data[id] : undefined; } };
Explanation: Provides a localStorage-compatible API using a simple object. Used when localStorage is blocked (private browsing, cookie restrictions) or unsupported (very old browsers).

Purpose: Manages game state persistence with dimension-aware keys.
Snippet: js function LocalStorageManager() { this.bestScoreKey = "bestScore"; this.gameStateKey = "gameState"; var supported = this.localStorageSupported(); this.storage = supported ? window.localStorage : window.fakeStorage; }
Explanation: Initializes with base key names. GameManager appends dimension suffix (e.g., gameState_4x4). Detects localStorage support via try/catch, falling back to fakeStorage if unavailable.

Purpose: Tests if localStorage is available and writable.
Snippet: js LocalStorageManager.prototype.localStorageSupported = function() { try { var storage = window.localStorage; storage.setItem(testKey, "1"); storage.removeItem(testKey); return true; } catch (error) { return false; } }
Explanation: Attempts to write and remove a test key. Returns false if any exception occurs (SecurityError, QuotaExceededError, etc.). This gracefully handles Safari private mode and other restrictive environments.

Purpose: Retrieves the persistent high score for specific grid size.
Snippet: js LocalStorageManager.prototype.getBestScore = function(key) { return this.storage.getItem(key || this.bestScoreKey) || 0; }
Explanation: Accepts optional key parameter for dimension-specific scores (e.g., bestScore_5x5). Defaults to 0 if not set. Called on game initialization and after every move.

Purpose: Saves the new high score for specific grid size.
Snippet: js LocalStorageManager.prototype.setBestScore = function(score, key) { this.storage.setItem(key || this.bestScoreKey, score); }
Explanation: Accepts optional key parameter for dimension-specific persistence. Uses the storage abstraction layer (localStorage or fakeStorage) for compatibility.

Purpose: Loads the serialized game state for specific dimensions.
Snippet: js LocalStorageManager.prototype.getGameState = function(key) { var stateJSON = this.storage.getItem(key || this.gameStateKey); return stateJSON ? JSON.parse(stateJSON) : null; }
Explanation: Accepts optional key parameter (e.g., gameState_4x4). Retrieves and deserializes the game state JSON. Returns null if no saved game exists. Allows resuming dimension-specific games.

Purpose: Saves the current game state as JSON with dimension key.
Snippet: js LocalStorageManager.prototype.setGameState = function(gameState, key) { this.storage.setItem(key || this.gameStateKey, JSON.stringify(gameState)); }
Explanation: Accepts optional key parameter for dimension-specific storage. Serializes the game state object (grid, score, won/over flags) and saves it. Called after every valid move.

Purpose: Removes the saved game from storage for specific dimensions.
Snippet: js LocalStorageManager.prototype.clearGameState = function(key) { this.storage.removeItem(key || this.gameStateKey); }
Explanation: Accepts optional key parameter. Deletes the dimension-specific saved game state. Called when the game ends (loss) or when user explicitly restarts.

Purpose: Creates a tile object with position and value.
Snippet: js function Tile(position, value) { this.x = position.x; this.y = position.y; this.value = value || 2; this.previousPosition = null; this.mergedFrom = null; }
Explanation: Initializes a tile at grid coordinates (x, y) with a default value of 2. previousPosition tracks animation state, mergedFrom stores the source tiles during merge animations.

Purpose: Stores current position before movement.
Snippet: js Tile.prototype.savePosition = function() { this.previousPosition = { x: this.x, y: this.y }; }
Explanation: Captures the tile's position before a move. The HTMLActuator uses this to render the sliding animation from previousPosition to the new position.

Purpose: Changes tile coordinates during movement.
Snippet: js Tile.prototype.updatePosition = function(position) { this.x = position.x; this.y = position.y; }
Explanation: Updates the tile's grid coordinates after a valid move. Called by the GameManager during tile sliding and merging operations.

Purpose: Converts tile to JSON-compatible object.
Snippet: js Tile.prototype.serialize = function() { return { position: { x: this.x, y: this.y }, value: this.value }; }
Explanation: Creates a plain object representation for storage. Only serializes essential data (position and value), omitting animation state like previousPosition and mergedFrom.

Purpose: Creates the variable-dimension cell structure.
Snippet: js function Grid(width, height, previousState) { this.width = width; this.height = height; this.cells = previousState ? this.fromState(previousState) : this.empty(); }
Explanation: Initializes either a fresh empty grid or restores a previous state from localStorage. Accepts separate width and height parameters (changed from single size parameter) to support non-square grids.

Purpose: Generates a blank 2D array with variable dimensions.
Snippet: js Grid.prototype.empty = function() { var cells = []; for (var x = 0; x < this.width; x++) { var row = cells[x] = []; for (var y = 0; y < this.height; y++) { row.push(null); } } return cells; }
Explanation: Creates a width×height matrix filled with null values. The structure is cells[x][y] where x is column and y is row. Null represents an empty cell with no tile.

Purpose: Reconstructs grid from saved game with dimension validation.
Snippet: js Grid.prototype.fromState = function(state) { var cells = []; for (var x = 0; x < this.width; x++) { var row = cells[x] = []; for (var y = 0; y < this.height; y++) { var tile = state[x][y]; row.push(tile ? new Tile(tile.position, tile.value) : null); } } return cells; }
Explanation: Deserializes the saved grid state, recreating Tile objects from plain JSON. Handles both occupied (create Tile) and empty (null) cells. Loops use this.width and this.height for variable dimensions.

Purpose: Selects a random empty cell for tile spawning.
Snippet: js Grid.prototype.randomAvailableCell = function() { var cells = this.availableCells(); if (cells.length) { return cells[Math.floor(Math.random() * cells.length)]; } }
Explanation: Calls availableCells() to get all empty positions, then returns a random one via Math.random(). Used after every valid move to spawn a new 2 or 4 tile.

Purpose: Returns array of all empty cell positions.
Snippet: js Grid.prototype.availableCells = function() { var cells = []; this.eachCell(function(x, y, tile) { if (!tile) { cells.push({ x: x, y: y }); } }); return cells; }
Explanation: Iterates through the entire grid and collects coordinates where tile is null. Essential for both random spawning and checking if moves are possible.

Purpose: Iterates over every cell with a callback using variable dimensions.
Snippet: js Grid.prototype.eachCell = function(callback) { for (var x = 0; x < this.width; x++) { for (var y = 0; y < this.height; y++) { callback(x, y, this.cells[x][y]); } } }
Explanation: Provides a clean abstraction for grid traversal. The callback receives (x, y, tile) for every cell. Loops iterate to this.width and this.height rather than fixed size.

Purpose: Boolean check if any empty cells exist.
Snippet: js Grid.prototype.cellsAvailable = function() { return !!this.availableCells().length; }
Explanation: Shorthand method returning true if at least one empty cell exists. The !! coerces the array length to a boolean. Used to determine if the game is lost.

Purpose: Retrieves the tile at specific coordinates.
Snippet: js Grid.prototype.cellContent = function(cell) { if (this.withinBounds(cell)) { return this.cells[cell.x][cell.y]; } else { return null; } }
Explanation: Safe accessor that checks bounds before returning. Returns null for out-of-bounds requests, preventing errors during edge detection in move logic.

Purpose: Validates if coordinates are inside the variable-dimension grid.
Snippet: js Grid.prototype.withinBounds = function(position) { return position.x >= 0 && position.x < this.width && position.y >= 0 && position.y < this.height; }
Explanation: Ensures x is between 0 and width-1, y between 0 and height-1. Updated to use this.width and this.height instead of fixed this.size for variable dimensions.

Purpose: Places a tile object into the grid.
Snippet: js Grid.prototype.insertTile = function(tile) { this.cells[tile.x][tile.y] = tile; }
Explanation: Assigns the tile reference to the appropriate cell. Called when spawning new tiles and when creating merged tiles during gameplay.

Purpose: Clears a cell by setting it to null.
Snippet: js Grid.prototype.removeTile = function(tile) { this.cells[tile.x][tile.y] = null; }
Explanation: Used during merges to remove the source tiles after creating the new merged tile. Also called during move operations to clear the previous position.

Purpose: Converts grid to JSON-compatible structure with dimensions.
Snippet: js Grid.prototype.serialize = function() { var cellState = []; for (var x = 0; x < this.width; x++) { var row = cellState[x] = []; for (var y = 0; y < this.height; y++) { row.push(this.cells[x][y] ? this.cells[x][y].serialize() : null); } } return { width: this.width, height: this.height, cells: cellState }; }
Explanation: Creates a plain object with grid dimensions (width, height) and serialized tiles. This structure can be JSON.stringify'd and saved to localStorage with dimension validation on load.

Purpose: Initializes DOM element references for rendering.
Snippet: js function HTMLActuator() { this.tileContainer = document.querySelector(".tile-container"); this.scoreContainer = document.querySelector(".score-container"); this.undoButton = document.getElementById("undo-button"); ... this.score = 0; }
Explanation: Caches references to key DOM elements for efficient updates. Now includes undoButton reference for enabling/disabling based on undo stack state.

Purpose: Master render method called after each move with grid rebuild.
Snippet: js HTMLActuator.prototype.actuate = function(grid, metadata) { var self = this; window.requestAnimationFrame(function() { self.clearContainer(self.tileContainer); self.updateGridSize(grid.width, grid.height); grid.cells.forEach(...); self.updateUndoButton(metadata.undoAvailable); }); }
Explanation: Wraps the entire render process in requestAnimationFrame for smooth 60 FPS updates. Now calls updateGridSize on every actuate to ensure grid visibility and correct positioning for current dimensions.

Purpose: Dynamically rebuilds grid and generates CSS for current dimensions.
Snippet: js HTMLActuator.prototype.updateGridSize = function(width, height) { var maxSize = 500; var gap = 15; var padding = 15; var availableSpace = maxSize - (2 * padding) - ((Math.max(width, height) - 1) * gap); var cellSize = Math.floor(availableSpace / Math.max(width, height)); cellSize = Math.max(cellSize, 40); ... }
Explanation: Calculates optimal cell size based on grid dimensions ensuring minimum 40px for touch targets. Rebuilds grid-container with new cell divs and injects dynamic CSS with position classes for every coordinate.

Purpose: Sets game container dimensions based on calculated cell size.
Snippet: js var containerWidth = (cellSize * width) + ((width - 1) * gap) + (2 * padding); var containerHeight = (cellSize * height) + ((height - 1) * gap) + (2 * padding); this.gameContainer.style.width = containerWidth + 'px'; this.gameContainer.style.height = containerHeight + 'px';
Explanation: Calculates exact container dimensions to fit the grid with proper spacing. Accounts for cell sizes, gaps (15px), and padding (15px) to ensure precise layout.

Purpose: Dynamically generates grid cell divs for current dimensions.
Snippet: js this.clearContainer(this.gridContainer); for (var y = 0; y < height; y++) { var row = document.createElement("div"); row.className = "grid-row"; for (var x = 0; x < width; x++) { var cell = document.createElement("div"); cell.className = "grid-cell"; cell.style.width = cellSize + 'px'; cell.style.height = cellSize + 'px'; row.appendChild(cell); } this.gridContainer.appendChild(row); }
Explanation: Clears existing grid cells and creates new divs matching current width×height. Each cell is sized precisely using calculated cellSize. Organized in rows for proper float layout.

Purpose: Runtime generation of tile positioning and sizing styles.
Snippet: js var styleId = 'dynamic-tile-styles'; var existingStyle = document.getElementById(styleId); if (existingStyle) { existingStyle.remove(); } var style = document.createElement('style'); style.id = styleId; var css = '.tile, .tile .tile-inner { width: ' + cellSize + 'px; ... }';
Explanation: Creates or replaces a style element with calculated CSS rules. Removes old styles to prevent accumulation. Injects tile dimensions, font sizes, and position classes into document.head.

Purpose: Scales font sizes proportionally to cell dimensions.
Snippet: js var fontSize = Math.floor(cellSize * 0.5); var smallFontSize = Math.floor(cellSize * 0.35); var tinyFontSize = Math.floor(cellSize * 0.25); css += '.tile .tile-inner { font-size: ' + fontSize + 'px; }'; css += '.tile.tile-128 .tile-inner, .tile.tile-256 .tile-inner, .tile.tile-512 .tile-inner { font-size: ' + smallFontSize + 'px; }';
Explanation: Dynamically calculates font sizes as percentages of cell size: base 50%, medium (128-512) 35%, tiny (1024-2048) 25%, super (2048+) 30%. Ensures readability across all grid sizes.

Purpose: Creates CSS transform rules for every grid coordinate.
Snippet: js for (var x = 0; x < width; x++) { for (var y = 0; y < height; y++) { var translateX = x * (cellSize + gap); var translateY = y * (cellSize + gap); css += '.tile.tile-position-' + (x + 1) + '-' + (y + 1) + ' { transform: translate(' + translateX + 'px, ' + translateY + 'px); }'; } }
Explanation: Generates position classes for every (x, y) coordinate in the grid. Calculates translate values based on cell size and gaps. Class names use 1-indexed coordinates (tile-position-1-1 through tile-position-W-H).

Purpose: Removes all child DOM elements.
Snippet: js HTMLActuator.prototype.clearContainer = function(container) { while (container.firstChild) { container.removeChild(container.firstChild); } }
Explanation: Efficiently clears the tile container by removing children one by one. Used before each render to prevent duplicate tile elements. Also used to clear grid-container before rebuilding.

Purpose: Creates and positions a tile DOM element.
Snippet: js HTMLActuator.prototype.addTile = function(tile, gridWidth, gridHeight) { var wrapper = document.createElement("div"); var inner = document.createElement("div"); var position = tile.previousPosition || { x: tile.x, y: tile.y }; var classes = ["tile", "tile-" + tile.value, positionClass]; ... }
Explanation: Constructs a two-div structure: wrapper for positioning/animation, inner for styling/number. Handles three states: new tiles (tile-new), moving tiles (previousPosition), and merged tiles (tile-merged with mergedFrom array).

Purpose: Sets CSS class attribute from array.
Snippet: js HTMLActuator.prototype.applyClasses = function(element, classes) { element.setAttribute("class", classes.join(" ")); }
Explanation: Converts an array of class names into a space-separated string. Used instead of classList because the implementation needs to replace all classes at once during animations.

Purpose: Generates CSS class for tile positioning.
Snippet: js HTMLActuator.prototype.positionClass = function(position) { position = this.normalizePosition(position); return "tile-position-" + position.x + "-" + position.y; }
Explanation: Creates class names like "tile-position-1-1" through "tile-position-W-H". Normalizes from 0-indexed (internal) to 1-indexed (CSS) coordinates. Works for any grid dimensions.

Purpose: Converts 0-indexed to 1-indexed coordinates.
Snippet: js HTMLActuator.prototype.normalizePosition = function(position) { return { x: position.x + 1, y: position.y + 1 }; }
Explanation: Adjusts coordinates from internal representation (0 to width-1) to CSS class naming convention (1 to width). This allows more intuitive CSS class names.

Purpose: Renders current score with delta animation.
Snippet: js HTMLActuator.prototype.updateScore = function(score) { this.clearContainer(this.scoreContainer); var difference = score - this.score; this.score = score; this.scoreContainer.textContent = this.score; if (difference > 0) { var addition = document.createElement("div"); addition.classList.add("score-addition"); addition.textContent = "+" + difference; this.scoreContainer.appendChild(addition); } }
Explanation: Clears previous content, displays new score, and creates a floating "+X" indicator if the score increased. The animation is handled by CSS (move-up keyframes).

Purpose: Displays the dimension-specific persistent high score.
Snippet: js HTMLActuator.prototype.updateBestScore = function(bestScore) { this.bestContainer.textContent = bestScore; }
Explanation: Simple text update to show the best score for current grid dimensions. Called every frame during gameplay, but only visually changes when a new high score is achieved for that dimension.

Purpose: Enables or disables undo button based on stack state.
Snippet: js HTMLActuator.prototype.updateUndoButton = function(undoAvailable) { this.undoButton.disabled = !undoAvailable; }
Explanation: Sets the disabled attribute on the undo button based on whether the undo stack has moves. When disabled, CSS styling (opacity: 0.5, cursor: not-allowed) provides visual feedback.

Purpose: Shows game-over or win overlay.
Snippet: js HTMLActuator.prototype.message = function(won) { var type = won ? "game-won" : "game-over"; var message = won ? "You win!" : "Game over!"; this.messageContainer.classList.add(type); this.messageContainer.getElementsByTagName("p")[0].textContent = message; }
Explanation: Adds the appropriate CSS class to trigger the fade-in animation and sets the message text. The game-won class enables the "Keep playing" button.

Purpose: Hides the game-over/win overlay.
Snippet: js HTMLActuator.prototype.clearMessage = function() { this.messageContainer.classList.remove("game-won"); this.messageContainer.classList.remove("game-over"); }
Explanation: Removes both possible message states. Called when restarting the game or choosing to continue after winning (keep playing button).

Purpose: Sets up input event handling with touch detection.
Snippet: js function KeyboardInputManager() { this.events = {}; if (window.navigator.msPointerEnabled) { this.eventTouchstart = "MSPointerDown"; ... } else { this.eventTouchstart = "touchstart"; ... } this.listen(); }
Explanation: Initializes the event emitter pattern and detects IE10's MSPointer events vs standard touch events. Immediately calls listen() to bind all input handlers.

Purpose: Registers event listeners.
Snippet: js KeyboardInputManager.prototype.on = function(event, callback) { if (!this.events[event]) { this.events[event] = []; } this.events[event].push(callback); }
Explanation: Implements a simple pub-sub pattern. The GameManager subscribes to "move", "restart", "keepPlaying", and "undo" events during initialization.

Purpose: Triggers registered event callbacks.
Snippet: js KeyboardInputManager.prototype.emit = function(event, data) { var callbacks = this.events[event]; if (callbacks) { callbacks.forEach(function(callback) { callback(data); }); } }
Explanation: Calls all registered callbacks for a given event name. Data parameter passes directional information (0-3) for move events or is unused for restart/keepPlaying/undo.

Purpose: Binds all input event handlers including undo shortcut.
Snippet: js KeyboardInputManager.prototype.listen = function() { var map = { 38: 0, 39: 1, 40: 2, 37: 3, 75: 0, 76: 1, 74: 2, 72: 3, 87: 0, 68: 1, 83: 2, 65: 3 }; document.addEventListener("keydown", function(event) { ... }); this.bindButtonPress("#undo-button", this.undo); ... }
Explanation: Maps keycodes to directions (0=up, 1=right, 2=down, 3=left), supports arrow keys, WASD, and Vim keys (HJKL). Now includes Ctrl/Cmd+Z detection for undo and binds undo button click event.

Purpose: Detects Ctrl+Z or Cmd+Z for undo functionality.
Snippet: js if ((event.ctrlKey || event.metaKey) && event.which === 90 && !event.shiftKey) { event.preventDefault(); self.emit("undo"); }
Explanation: Checks for Ctrl (Windows/Linux) or Cmd (macOS) key combined with Z key (keycode 90). Ensures Shift is not pressed to differentiate from redo. Prevents default browser behavior and emits undo event.

Purpose: Translates key codes to move directions.
Explanation: Arrow keys (37-40), Vim navigation (HJKL: 72,74,75,76), and WASD (65,68,83,87) all map to the same four directional values. This ensures accessibility across different user preferences.

Purpose: Prevents conflicts with browser shortcuts.
Snippet: js var modifiers = event.altKey || event.ctrlKey || event.metaKey || event.shiftKey; if (!modifiers) { if (mapped !== undefined) { event.preventDefault(); self.emit("move", mapped); } }
Explanation: Only processes game inputs when no modifier keys are pressed (except for undo which explicitly checks for Ctrl/Cmd). This prevents accidentally interfering with Ctrl+R (refresh), Cmd+W (close tab), etc.

Purpose: Converts swipes to directional moves.
Snippet: js var dx = touchEndClientX - touchStartClientX; var absDx = Math.abs(dx); var dy = touchEndClientY - touchStartClientY; var absDy = Math.abs(dy); if (Math.max(absDx, absDy) > 10) { self.emit("move", absDx > absDy ? (dx > 0 ? 1 : 3) : (dy > 0 ? 2 : 0)); }
Explanation: Calculates horizontal and vertical deltas between touchstart and touchend. Requires 10px minimum movement to prevent accidental triggers. Emits move event based on dominant axis and direction.

Purpose: Handles restart button clicks.
Snippet: js KeyboardInputManager.prototype.restart = function(event) { event.preventDefault(); this.emit("restart"); }
Explanation: Prevents default anchor behavior and emits the restart event. Subscribed by GameManager to clear state and set up a new game.

Purpose: Allows continuing after winning.
Snippet: js KeyboardInputManager.prototype.keepPlaying = function(event) { event.preventDefault(); this.emit("keepPlaying"); }
Explanation: Enables post-2048 gameplay when user clicks "Keep going" button. Emits keepPlaying event which sets the keepPlaying flag in GameManager.

Purpose: Handles undo button clicks and keyboard shortcut.
Snippet: js KeyboardInputManager.prototype.undo = function(event) { event.preventDefault(); this.emit("undo"); }
Explanation: Prevents default behavior and emits the undo event. Triggered by either clicking the undo button or pressing Ctrl/Cmd+Z. GameManager subscribes to restore previous state.

Purpose: Attaches click and touch handlers to buttons.
Snippet: js KeyboardInputManager.prototype.bindButtonPress = function(selector, fn) { var button = document.querySelector(selector); button.addEventListener("click", fn.bind(this)); button.addEventListener(this.eventTouchend, fn.bind(this)); }
Explanation: Binds both mouse clicks and touch events to ensure buttons work on all devices. Uses .bind(this) to preserve context when callbacks are invoked. Now used for restart, retry, keep-playing, and undo buttons.

Purpose: Initializes the core game controller with dimension-specific keys.
Snippet: js function GameManager(width, height, InputManager, Actuator, StorageManager) { this.width = width; this.height = height; ... this.undoStack = []; this.bestScoreKey = 'bestScore_' + width + 'x' + height; this.gameStateKey = 'gameState_' + width + 'x' + height; this.inputManager.on("undo", this.undo.bind(this)); this.setup(); }
Explanation: Creates instances of all manager classes and subscribes to input events including new undo event. Initializes empty undoStack array. Creates dimension-specific storage keys (e.g., bestScore_4x4, gameState_5x5).

Purpose: Initializes or restores game state with dimension validation.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.setup = function() { var previousState = this.storageManager.getGameState(this.gameStateKey); if (previousState && previousState.grid && previousState.grid.width === this.width && previousState.grid.height === this.height) { this.grid = new Grid(previousState.grid.width, previousState.grid.height, previousState.grid.cells); ... } else { this.storageManager.clearGameState(this.gameStateKey); this.grid = new Grid(this.width, this.height); ... this.addStartTiles(); } this.actuate(); }
Explanation: Checks for saved game state with strict dimension validation. Only restores if state exists AND dimensions match exactly. If dimensions mismatch, clears incompatible state and starts fresh game with 2 random tiles.

Purpose: Spawns initial 2 or 4 tiles.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.addStartTiles = function() { for (var i = 0; i < this.startTiles; i++) { this.addRandomTile(); } }
Explanation: Loops startTiles times (2) to spawn the initial tiles. Each call to addRandomTile() has a 90/10 chance of creating a 2 or 4.

Purpose: Creates a new tile in an empty cell.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.addRandomTile = function() { if (this.grid.cellsAvailable()) { var value = Math.random() < 0.9 ? 2 : 4; var tile = new Tile(this.grid.randomAvailableCell(), value); this.grid.insertTile(tile); } }
Explanation: Only spawns if empty cells exist (prevents errors). 90% chance of value=2, 10% chance of value=4. Uses randomAvailableCell() to pick position, then inserts the new tile.

Purpose: Syncs state to dimension-specific storage and triggers render.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.actuate = function() { var currentBest = this.storageManager.getBestScore(this.bestScoreKey); if (currentBest < this.score) { this.storageManager.setBestScore(this.score, this.bestScoreKey); } if (this.over) { this.storageManager.clearGameState(this.gameStateKey); } else { this.storageManager.setGameState(this.serialize(), this.gameStateKey); } this.actuator.actuate(this.grid, { ... undoAvailable: this.undoStack.length > 0 }); }
Explanation: Updates dimension-specific best score if exceeded, saves/clears game state based on over flag using dimension-specific key. Passes undoAvailable flag (boolean) to actuator for button state management.

Purpose: Converts game state to JSON-compatible object.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.serialize = function() { return { grid: this.grid.serialize(), score: this.score, over: this.over, won: this.won, keepPlaying: this.keepPlaying }; }
Explanation: Creates a plain object with grid (including width/height) and all game flags. Used by setGameState() to save to localStorage. The grid.serialize() call handles the nested tile serialization with dimensions.

Purpose: Captures current game state into undo stack.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.saveState = function() { this.undoStack.push({ grid: this.grid.serialize(), score: this.score, over: this.over, won: this.won }); if (this.undoStack.length > 50) { this.undoStack.shift(); } }
Explanation: Serializes current game state (grid, score, game flags) and pushes to undo stack. Implements 50-move limit via FIFO pruning (shift removes oldest) to prevent unbounded memory growth.

Purpose: Restores previous game state from undo stack.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.undo = function() { if (this.undoStack.length === 0) return; var previousState = this.undoStack.pop(); this.grid = new Grid(previousState.grid.width, previousState.grid.height, previousState.grid.cells); this.score = previousState.score; this.over = previousState.over; this.won = previousState.won; this.actuate(); }
Explanation: Returns early if stack is empty. Pops most recent state, deserializes grid creating new Grid instance with Tile objects. Restores score and game flags. Calls actuate to re-render with restored state.

Purpose: Saves positions and clears merge flags.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.prepareTiles = function() { this.grid.eachCell(function(x, y, tile) { if (tile) { tile.mergedFrom = null; tile.savePosition(); } }); }
Explanation: Called before processing moves. Clears mergedFrom flags (prevents tiles from merging twice in one move) and saves current positions for animation.

Purpose: Relocates a tile in the grid.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.moveTile = function(tile, cell) { this.grid.cells[tile.x][tile.y] = null; this.grid.cells[cell.x][cell.y] = tile; tile.updatePosition(cell); }
Explanation: Clears the tile's old cell, assigns it to the new cell, and updates the tile's internal coordinates. Used when a tile slides without merging.

Purpose: Processes directional input and updates state with undo support.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.move = function(direction) { var self = this; if (this.isGameTerminated()) return; var vector = this.getVector(direction); var traversals = this.buildTraversals(vector); var moved = false; this.saveState(); this.prepareTiles(); traversals.x.forEach(...); if (moved) { this.addRandomTile(); if (!this.movesAvailable()) { this.over = true; } this.actuate(); } else { this.undoStack.pop(); } }
Explanation: Core game logic: saves state to undo stack BEFORE processing move, saves tile positions, traverses grid in direction order, moves/merges tiles, spawns new tile if anything moved. If no movement occurred, removes the saved state from undo stack.

Purpose: Converts direction to x/y offsets.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.getVector = function(direction) { var map = { 0: { x: 0, y: -1 }, 1: { x: 1, y: 0 }, 2: { x: 0, y: 1 }, 3: { x: -1, y: 0 } }; return map[direction]; }
Explanation: Maps directional constants to coordinate deltas. 0=up (y-1), 1=right (x+1), 2=down (y+1), 3=left (x-1). Used to calculate next cell positions during movement.

Purpose: Orders grid traversal based on direction and grid dimensions.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.buildTraversals = function(vector) { var traversals = { x: [], y: [] }; for (var pos = 0; pos < this.width; pos++) { traversals.x.push(pos); } for (var pos = 0; pos < this.height; pos++) { traversals.y.push(pos); } if (vector.x === 1) traversals.x = traversals.x.reverse(); if (vector.y === 1) traversals.y = traversals.y.reverse(); return traversals; }
Explanation: Creates arrays [0...width-1] for x and [0...height-1] for y. Reverses the order if moving right (x=1) or down (y=1). This ensures tiles are processed from the far edge toward the direction of movement.

Purpose: Calculates how far a tile can slide.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.findFarthestPosition = function(cell, vector) { var previous; do { previous = cell; cell = { x: previous.x + vector.x, y: previous.y + vector.y }; } while (this.grid.withinBounds(cell) && this.grid.cellAvailable(cell)); return { farthest: previous, next: cell }; }
Explanation: Iteratively steps in the direction vector until hitting a boundary or occupied cell. Returns both the last valid position (farthest) and the blocking cell (next) for merge detection.

Purpose: Combines two tiles of equal value.
Snippet: js if (next && next.value === tile.value && !next.mergedFrom) { var merged = new Tile(positions.next, tile.value * 2); merged.mergedFrom = [tile, next]; self.grid.insertTile(merged); self.grid.removeTile(tile); tile.updatePosition(positions.next); self.score += merged.value; if (merged.value === 2048) self.won = true; }
Explanation: Checks if the blocking cell contains a tile with the same value and hasn't merged yet. Creates a new tile with double value, marks it with source tiles for animation, removes originals, updates score, and checks for win condition (2048).

Purpose: Determines if any valid moves exist.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.movesAvailable = function() { return this.grid.cellsAvailable() || this.tileMatchesAvailable(); }
Explanation: Returns true if there are empty cells OR if any adjacent tiles can merge. Used to detect game-over state (returns false when grid is full and no merges possible).

Purpose: Checks for adjacent matching tiles in variable-dimension grid.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.tileMatchesAvailable = function() { var self = this; var tile; for (var x = 0; x < this.width; x++) { for (var y = 0; y < this.height; y++) { tile = this.grid.cellContent({ x: x, y: y }); if (tile) { for (var direction = 0; direction < 4; direction++) { var vector = self.getVector(direction); var cell = { x: x + vector.x, y: y + vector.y }; var other = self.grid.cellContent(cell); if (other && other.value === tile.value) { return true; } } } } } return false; }
Explanation: Iterates through every occupied cell (using this.width and this.height) and checks all four adjacent cells for matching values. Returns true immediately upon finding a match. Only called when grid is full.

Purpose: Compares two coordinate objects.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.positionsEqual = function(first, second) { return first.x === second.x && first.y === second.y; }
Explanation: Simple equality check for position objects. Used to determine if a tile actually moved (if position hasn't changed, moved flag stays false and undo state is removed).

Purpose: Resets the game to initial state clearing undo stack.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.restart = function() { this.storageManager.clearGameState(this.gameStateKey); this.actuator.continueGame(); this.undoStack = []; this.setup(); }
Explanation: Deletes dimension-specific saved game, clears the win/loss overlay, empties the undo stack, and calls setup() to create a fresh game with 2 starting tiles.

Purpose: Allows post-win gameplay.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.keepPlaying = function() { this.keepPlaying = true; this.actuator.continueGame(); }
Explanation: Sets the keepPlaying flag to true and hides the win message. This prevents isGameTerminated() from returning true, allowing continued play after reaching 2048.

Purpose: Checks if gameplay should be blocked.
Snippet: js GameManager.prototype.isGameTerminated = function() { return this.over || (this.won && !this.keepPlaying); }
Explanation: Returns true if game is lost OR if won without choosing to continue. Used in move() to prevent input processing during game-over/win states.

Purpose: Controller for creating new game instances with dimension management.
Snippet: js function initGame(width, height) { if (currentGame) { currentGame.inputManager.events = {}; } currentGame = new GameManager(width, height, KeyboardInputManager, HTMLActuator, LocalStorageManager); document.getElementById('current-grid-size').textContent = width + '×' + height; }
Explanation: Cleans up previous game instance by clearing event listeners to prevent memory leaks. Creates new GameManager with specified dimensions. Updates grid size display indicator with dimension string (e.g., "4×4").

Purpose: Binds click handlers to preset grid size buttons.
Snippet: js var presetButtons = document.querySelectorAll('.preset-btn'); presetButtons.forEach(function(btn) { btn.addEventListener('click', function() { var width = parseInt(this.getAttribute('data-width')); var height = parseInt(this.getAttribute('data-height')); presetButtons.forEach(function(b) { b.classList.remove('active'); }); this.classList.add('active'); document.getElementById('custom-width').value = width; document.getElementById('custom-height').value = height; initGame(width, height); }); });
Explanation: Selects all preset buttons and attaches click listeners. Extracts dimensions from data attributes, updates active visual state (removes from all, adds to clicked), syncs custom input fields, and initializes new game with selected dimensions.

Purpose: Processes custom dimension input and validates range.
Snippet: js document.getElementById('apply-custom').addEventListener('click', function() { var width = parseInt(document.getElementById('custom-width').value); var height = parseInt(document.getElementById('custom-height').value); width = Math.max(3, Math.min(10, width)); height = Math.max(3, Math.min(10, height)); document.getElementById('custom-width').value = width; document.getElementById('custom-height').value = height; presetButtons.forEach(function(b) { b.classList.remove('active'); }); initGame(width, height); });
Explanation: Reads width and height from input fields as integers. Clamps values to 3-10 range using Math.max/min. Updates input fields with clamped values for visual feedback. Clears preset button active states. Initializes new game with custom dimensions.

Purpose: Starts the default 4×4 game on page load.
Snippet: js window.requestAnimationFrame(function() { initGame(4, 4); });
Explanation: Waits for next animation frame to ensure DOM is fully loaded, then creates the initial GameManager instance with 4×4 grid dimensions. This single line launches the entire game with default configuration.
